champion.description: B46 shared-Haar signed-orbit radial-exact Monte Carlo: one sign-corrected exact-Haar frame supplies 25 blocks through 24 independent seeded Rademacher column diagonals, followed by 100 iid normalized directions. Every row remains exactly uniform on the sphere, preserving unbiasedness while replacing B43's 25 QR factorizations with one. The directions use B42's float32 650-row chunked forward, float64 accumulation, and closed-form chi(width) mean-radius scaling.
champion.note: Promoted after the B46 standard Mini paired gate: adjusted score -29.81%, final-layer MSE -30.42%, paired adjusted-score CI entirely below zero, and zero failure flags. The effect is statistically distinct from B42 and requires a fresh independent Full paired gate before any submission.

previous_champion.submitted: S3-claude-lead-20260716T171600Z (submission_id 316676, graded; leaderboard 6.684538479656953e-07; local Full 8.507033588741281e-07 = last_submitted_score)

full_gate.method: B26: reused B24's chunked/resumable method (whestbench.cli._run_estimator_with_runner driven directly over explicit index ranges of the immutable Full split, ground truth read from the dataset's precomputed fields, never recomputed) for the new B25 champion. Ten independent 100-MLP subprocess-runner chunks (indices 0-99 through 900-999, zero overlap, zero duplicates, verified via globally-unique mlp_name across all chunks) run sequentially in the foreground -- smaller than B24's two 500-MLP chunks because concurrent/backgrounded chunk runs were unreliable in this session (killed partway through with no output, both with and without a scheduled wakeup in between; single foreground chunks of 100 MLPs, ~5 minutes each, completed reliably every time). MANDATORY correctness check before trusting the chunked path (per B24's precedent, redone because the estimator is new): cross-checked against the official `whest run` CLI on 20 Full-split MLPs and 30 Mini-split MLPs -- aggregate final_layer_mse matched the CLI to the exact last printed digit in both checks. CRITICAL bug caught and fixed by that same check: an initial version called whestbench.scoring.make_contest_from_dataset on a pre-sliced dataset (ds.select(range(start,end))), which silently lost the weakref metadata side-channel (a new Dataset object is not a key in whestbench's WeakKeyDictionary metadata store) and fell back to the wrong default seed_protocol_version ("2.0" instead of this dataset's real "3.0"), corrupting MLP reconstruction -- confirmed empirically (final_layer_mse diverged by up to ~6x per MLP from the CLI despite identical flops_used and clean budget/time flags on both sides). Fixed by reading seed_protocol_version from whestbench.metadata() on the ORIGINAL unsliced dataset before slicing, then building ContestData manually with the correct protocol version; the fix was reverified against the CLI (exact-digit match) before running any of the ten real chunks.
full_gate.note: Full-split score (final_layer_mse=7.6925e-06) is consistent with, and marginally better than, the Mini-split champion numbers (7.2108e-06 mini vs 7.6925e-06 full -- within the expected suite-sampling range, not a large divergence) -- no evidence of Mini-split overfitting. Sanity check: the combined 1000-MLP aggregate's final_layer_mse matches the unweighted mean of the ten chunks' own independently-reported final_layer_mse values to 10 significant figures, confirming correct combination. This was a single-estimator Full-split score, not a paired comparison -- B29 (2026-07-16T18:30:00Z) has since computed the paired Full-split comparison against the B0 champion using this report plus B0's own complete Full-split report from B24; see submission_readiness below for the result.

previous_champion.note: B25 radial-exact champion record preserved on supersession by B42. B42 changes NO statistics (same seeded draw, same directions; predictions within 7.7e-08); its full_gate above therefore characterizes the new champion's MSE exactly, but the new champion's own Full-split multiplier/effective-compute record must still be measured fresh before any submission (AGENTS.md step 7).

full_gate.method: B47: ran the B46 champion (estimator.py, shared-Haar signed-orbit) on the complete 1000-MLP Full split via B26's validated chunked driver (seed_protocol_version fix; ten 100-MLP subprocess chunks). Paired per-MLP final_layer_mse AND adjusted_final_layer_score by mlp_name against B26's complete champion Full report (estimator.py @ 2227ef3 = the submitted B25 baseline; B42's residual-min changed compute not predictions, so B26's MSE is the exact B25/B42 baseline). Motivated by a real risk: B46 uses a SIGN-based construction (Rademacher column diagonals on one shared Haar frame) that B31 flagged as potentially depth-32-fragile, and B44 showed the Mini gate is unreliable for orthogonal effects -- so B46's Mini-only promotion needed independent Full confirmation.

paired_vs_submitted_B25_baseline.broad_not_outlier_driven: MSE delta survives excl-top-2 (-1.487e-06) and the median delta is -6.37e-07 (typical MLP improves)

full_gate.note: PASS. B46's orthogonal-directions MSE benefit HOLDS robustly on the independent Full split (the sign-orbit construction is NOT depth-32-fragile; B31 concern does not apply here), and matches B43's independent-QR construction (B46 Full MSE 6.079e-06 vs B43 6.051e-06, +0.5% -- statistically identical) at ~1/25 the QR cost. Both paired gates (MSE -5.4 sigma, adjusted -6.0 sigma) have CIs entirely below zero. B46's Full adjusted score 6.533e-07 improves on last_submitted_score 8.507e-07 by -23.2%, far above the 5% bar. AGENTS.md step 7's Full-gate and 5%-improvement prerequisites are SATISFIED for a B46 submission (a reservation + network submit is the remaining action; see submission_readiness).

lead_ruling.rationale: B29 escalated for an explicit lead/user ruling rather than waive step 7 unilaterally. Ruling: the S1 addendum's second clause ('if all previous submitted scores are null, then any scoring solution can be submitted') is a positive permission covering the first scored submission. The paired Full gate protects a previously-submitted baseline, and none exists: B0 was never submitted and both 2026-06-11 pre-scaffold artifacts are null-scored. B25 is not-worse than B0 on Full (B29: paired mean -8.67e-09, aggregate -1.14%), has a COMPLETE 1000/1000 Full-split record (B26, zero failure flags), and validates cleanly. The unchanged reservation protocol governs execution. Full reasoning: experiments/log-claude-lead.md, Lead review 2026-07-16T17:08:00Z. Queued as backlog item S3.

paired_full_split_gate.result: FAIL -- CI upper bound (+1.118e-08) is positive, does not clear zero. Verified robust to t_crit choice (1.96 to 2.0, all same conclusion).
paired_full_split_gate.note: B25's paired advantage over B0 was real and significant on the 100-MLP Mini split at promotion time (CI=[-2.581e-07,-2.868e-08]) but shrinks to statistically-indistinguishable-from-zero on the 10x-larger, independent Full split -- exactly the suite-sampling-overfitting risk AGENTS.md's two-tier Mini/Full gate design exists to catch. Does not invalidate B25's promotion (which correctly used the Mini-split-only promotion gate); B25 remains champion. Aggregate final_layer_mse still improves on Full (-1.14%), just not enough to clear the paired significance bar.

submission_readiness.next_steps: Run B46 on the independent Full split and compare per MLP against the exact submitted S3/B25 baseline under the same environment contract. Reserve and submit only if the paired Full CI is entirely negative, the local Full score is at least 5% below last_submitted_score, all flags are zero, and no active submitting reservation exists.

noise_floor.method: The dataset's ground-truth `final_means`/`all_layer_means` are themselves a Monte Carlo estimate (whestbench.scoring.sample_layer_statistics), so they carry their own sampling noise -- a lower bound on how low any estimator's final_layer_mse could ever go. Calibrated FLOPs-per-sample for sample_layer_statistics directly via flopscope.budget() at n_samples=1,000,000 (large enough that chunking/fixed-overhead effects are negligible; measured 4,632,529.741312 FLOPs/sample, converging from smaller-n estimates at n=500/2000/5000 which showed some chunking-related variance). Backed out the implied ground-truth sample count n_gt = row.sampling_budget_breakdown.flops_used / flops_per_sample for all 100 Mini-split MLPs; identical for every row (909,050,195 samples, ~909 million), consistent with a fixed generation policy (same width=256, same target flop_budget=1e15 for every Phase 1 MLP). Combined with each row's own avg_variance (the mean per-neuron single-sample variance at the final layer, already stored per-row, not the same quantity as an estimator's own MC variance) via the standard MC variance-of-mean formula Var(ground-truth mean) = avg_variance / n_gt.
noise_floor.comparison: Champion's current final_layer_mse is 7.211e-06 (mini, B25) / 7.693e-06 (full, B26) -- the ground-truth noise floor (~1.7e-11 to 2.2e-10, mean 5.4e-11) is roughly 5-6 orders of magnitude (100,000x to 1,000,000x) smaller. The ground truth is effectively exact at any precision level current or plausible future estimators could reach.
noise_floor.conclusion: No meaningful noise floor limits further estimator improvement. The gap between any estimator's final_layer_mse and zero is essentially entirely attributable to the estimator's own sampling variance, not ground-truth imprecision. Further MSE reductions (if a valid mechanism is found) would show up as genuine score improvements, not be swamped by irreducible ground-truth noise.
noise_floor.raw_computation: Not persisted as a separate raw report -- this is a closed-form calculation from already-public dataset fields (sampling_budget_breakdown, avg_variance) plus a one-off flopscope calibration measurement, not a harness run. Full derivation and calibration values in experiments/log-claude.md's B28 entry.

submissions.grading_message: Error : Evaluation error (score null). The submission was ACCEPTED (id 316800) but the AICrowd grader errored during evaluation. NOT auto-retried per AGENTS.md. last_submitted_score UNCHANGED (still 8.507033588741281e-07 from S3). LIKELY CAUSE: B46 uses fnp.linalg.qr / fnp.sign / fnp.diagonal / fnp.concatenate (the orthogonal-block construction); the prior successful submission S3 (B25 radial-exact) used NONE of these -- only standard_normal/matmul/maximum/mean/norm/stack. The grader's evaluation environment may not support fnp.linalg.qr (or a related op) even though it runs cleanly locally on 1000 MLPs. NEEDS INVESTIGATION before any resubmission: (1) check whether fnp.linalg.qr is a supported/gradeable op (whest doctor / validate-package / the harness's supported-op registry), (2) if not, the orthogonal-directions champions (B43/B46) are locally-superior but UNGRADEABLE, and a QR-free equivalent (or reverting the submitted artifact to the gradeable B25/B42 while keeping the local champion) must be decided by the lead/user.
